Document:
# Big Guns
[textlink="View Full Wiki Page" link="https://ss14.misfitsystems.net/wiki/index.php/Big_Guns"]
Big Guns are heavy weapons that deal devastating damage. They are difficult to carry and expensive to feed, but nothing matches their raw stopping power once a fight turns serious.
## Miniguns
Miniguns deliver overwhelming volume of fire and are best used for suppression, defense, and faction-level fire superiority.
[bold]Minigun[/bold] — Standard rotary-barrel machine gun. Enormous ammo consumption but unmatched sustained fire.
[bold]Avenger Minigun[/bold] — Military-upgraded minigun with a higher rate of fire and improved accuracy.
## Launchers
Grenade and missile launchers are area-denial weapons that break fortified positions and punish clustered enemies.
[bold]Grenade Launcher[/bold] — Fires explosive grenades in an arc. Effective against groups and behind cover.
[bold]Missile Launcher[/bold] — Shoulder-fired guided missile platform. Devastating but expensive to reload.
[bold]Rifle Grenade[/bold] — Grenade attachment fired from a rifle. Lower yield but more portable.
## Light Machine Guns
LMGs provide sustained automatic fire and excel in prepared positions or coordinated squad fights.
[bold]Light Machine Gun[/bold] — Belt-fed automatic weapon. Best used from a stationary or braced position.
[bold]Automatic Rifle[/bold] — Magazine-fed support weapon. Lighter than a proper LMG and easier to reposition.
## Flamers
The flamer projects burning fuel across a wide area and is especially effective against creatures and lightly armored targets.
[bold]Flamer[/bold] — Short-range incendiary weapon. Area denial and crowd control in one package.
[bold][color=red]Warning:[/color][/bold] Big Guns are usually restricted to military-grade factions. Carrying one openly is enough to attract attention almost anywhere in the Wasteland.
